items.title: On Easter, Pope Leo urges world leaders to end wars, conquest
items.summary_auto: Pope Leo urged global leaders in his Easter message on Sunday to end the conflicts raging across the world and abandon any schemes for power, conquest or domination.

items.title: Israeli airstrike kills 4 Palestinians in Gaza, medics say
items.summary_auto: An Israeli airstrike killed four Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip on Sunday, local health authorities said, in the latest violence to overshadow ‌a fragile ceasefire amid a new push by mediators to bolster the agreement.

items.title: Bogus maple syrup and WestJet under fire for flight cancellations: CBC's Marketplace Cheat Sheet
items.summary_auto: The CBC's Marketplace rounds up the consumer and health news you need from the week.

items.title: Blinded by the headlights? Transport Canada wants to know
items.summary_auto: A London, Ont., optometrist says she's hearing more concerns from patients about  difficulty driving in the dark and headlight brightness. Transport Canada is asking drivers, pedestrians and cyclists to share their experiences with vehicle headlight glare through an online survey.

items.title: COVID-19 wreaked havoc on this N.S. seniors' group. It's now trying to rebuild
items.summary_auto: An organization that has advocated for Nova Scotia seniors for more than five decades says there's a sad reason why its membership numbers have dropped sharply in recent years. "We lost a lot of people through COVID," says the president of the Nova Scotia Federation of Seniors.

items.title: Kadin Savikataaq has been missing for a year and a half. Her family is still searching
items.summary_auto: On Aug. 19, 2024, 20-year-old Kadin Savikataaq went out for the night and never returned. The truck she'd been driving was discovered submerged in Hudson Bay, but a year and a half later, Kadin is still missing. RCMP say she likely drowned, but her family doesn't agree. They say they'll never stop looking for her.

items.title: Influencers tout injectable peptides as key to youth and beauty. Scientists say they're 'a giant scam'
items.summary_auto: Crocodile dung. Tapeworms. Raw meat facials. Yes, at some point in history these wildly popular — and wildly unproven — treatments were how people tried to stay young. Right now, plenty of influencers will tell you that injectable peptides are the answer. But the scientists? Not so much.

items.title: Ban on rescue dogs from countries at risk of spreading rabies to remain, CFIA says
items.summary_auto: The federal government says a ban on the import of rescue dogs into Canada will remain in place until further notice due to rabies concerns. Animal advocacy groups say the restrictions — which were brought in more than three years ago and originally meant to be temporary — are preventing healthy animals from legitimate international rescues from finding homes in Canada.

items.title: Private collection of centuries-old Roman sculptures on display in Montreal
items.summary_auto: In the 19th century, Italian prince, banker and collector Alessandro Torlonia, gathered 623 ancient Roman sculptures. After being shown at the Louvre in Paris, the Torlonia Collection has touched down in Montreal, where it will remain on view at the Museum of Fine Arts until July 19.

items.title: Amid political tensions, King Charles will be in a 'delicate diplomatic situation' during U.S. state visit
items.summary_auto: The timing of King Charles's upcoming state visit to the United States has sparked controversy and questions — particularly given the ongoing U.S.-Israel war against Iran, the geopolitical turmoil it has caused and the current strain in the relationship between the United Kingdom and the U.S.

items.title: Injectable peptides touted as new fountain of youth. But the science isn't there
items.summary_auto: Crocodile dung. Tapeworms. Raw meat facials. Yes, at some point in history these wildly popular — and wildly unproven — treatments were how people tried to stay young. Right now, plenty of influencers will tell you that injectable peptides are the answer. But the scientists? Not so much.

items.title: Project Hail Mary and Artemis II's launch reflect a long parallel between NASA and Hollywood
items.summary_auto: Artemis II's launch and Project Hail Mary's stunning success reflect a long line of films about space — films that similarly released alongside real life space-exploration projects, or increased public fascination with the subject.

items.title: Tumbler Ridge, B.C., runner-up in Hockeyville contest; Taber, Alta., wins
items.summary_auto: Taber, Alta., has won this year’s Kraft Hockeyville contest, with Tumbler Ridge, B.C., finishing runner-up.

items.title: Houston, we've got a plumbing problem: Artemis II toilet is still on the fritz
items.summary_auto: Until the bathroom is fixed, mission control has instructed the astronauts to break out urine collection bags. The so-called lunar loo malfunctioned following Wednesday's liftoff and has been hit-and-miss ever since.

items.title: Canada falls to Sweden 9-6 in final of men’s curling worlds
items.summary_auto: Canada’s Matt Dunstone fell 9-6 to Sweden in Saturday’s final of the 2026 World Men’s Curling Championship. Canada has not won the men’s world title since 2017.

items.title: CFIA announces recalls for salads, cheeses and meal kits over listeria concerns
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency has announced recalls due to possible listeria contamination affecting products including salads, cheese and meal kits.
